% Auto-generated theorem box for LP-0018
\begin{theorem}[LP-0018]
The polynomial continued fraction with
$a(n) = -2.0*n**2$ and $b(n) = 3.0*(1.0*n + 0.666666666666667)$
converges to $1/ln2$.
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
Closed forms: $p_n = (n+1)! * 2.0^{n+1}$, $q_n = (n+1)! * sum_{j=0}^n 2.0^{n-j}/(j+1)$. Verified by induction against the recurrence. Limit: sum x^j/(j+1) = -ln(1-x)/x.
\end{proof}
\begin{corollary}[Arb Certification]
At depth $N$, the bracket width is $< [1.973185316278960272634076658$,
certifying the identity to $1208$ decimal digits.
\end{corollary}
